commands.author: NossieUK
commands.command: — with respect, the source-code review is not a substitute for runtime verification. our concrete trace logs from 5.3-beta.4 on a warm pi 5 show `loaded 92 plugin(s) (66 attempted)` on **every single agent turn**: ``` turn 1: loaded 92 plugin(s) (66 attempted) in 1389.4ms turn 2: loaded 92 plugin(s) (66 attempted) in 993.8ms turn 3: loaded 92 plugin(s) (66 attempted) in 1092.2ms ``` if the descriptor cache and factory-gating you reviewed were fully effective, we would see **zero** plugin load messages on warm turns, or at minimum a single cache-hit log. instead we see 92 plugin factories being attempted every turn. the regression tests you cite prove that a **single test-case** can skip factory calls on identical context. our production trace proves that **real embedded runs on warm sessions** still invoke the full factory path. please re-open this issue. the architectural fix is not proven until a warm embedded run shows `core-plugin-tools < 100ms` without any plugin load messages.
commands.freeform_prompt: — with respect, the source-code review is not a substitute for runtime verification. Our concrete trace logs from 5.3-beta.4 on a warm Pi 5 show `loaded 92 plugin(s) (66 attempted)` on **every single agent turn**: ``` Turn 1: loaded 92 plugin(s) (66 attempted) in 1389.4ms Turn 2: loaded 92 plugin(s) (66 attempted) in 993.8ms Turn 3: loaded 92 plugin(s) (66 attempted) in 1092.2ms ``` If the descriptor cache and factory-gating you reviewed were fully effective, we would see **zero** plugin load messages on warm turns, or at minimum a single cache-hit log. Instead we see 92 plugin factories being attempted every turn. The regression tests you cite prove that a **single test-case** can skip factory calls on identical context. Our production trace proves that **real embedded runs on warm sessions** still invoke the full factory path. Please re-open this issue. The architectural fix is not proven until a warm embedded run shows `core-plugin-tools < 100ms` without any plugin load messages.
commands.reason: author association NONE and repository permission read are not allowed
